./.title: Home
./.body:
GraphQL API Reference
This folder receives generated markdown files from GraphQL-Markdown.

Local schema output: docs/graphql
Remote schema output: docs/graphql-remote

Import this docs directory into GitBook.com after running one of the generate scripts.
Local Schema
Local Operations
Local Object
Local Input
Local Enum
Local Scalar
Local Directive
Local Other
Remote Schema
Remote Operations
Remote Object
Remote Input
Remote Enum
Remote Scalar
Remote Directive
Remote Other

graphql/create-issue.html.title: createIssue
graphql/create-issue.html.body:
createIssue mutation

  On this page
  ArgumentsType





Create a new issue in a repository.
createIssue(
  input: CreateIssueInput!
): Issue!

Arguments
createIssue.input ● CreateIssueInput! non-null input
Type
Issue object

graphql/repositories.html.title: repositories
graphql/repositories.html.body:
repositories query

  On this page
  ArgumentsType





List repositories with optional paging.
repositories(
  first: Int = 10
): [Repository!]!

Arguments
repositories.first ● Int scalar
Type
Repository object

Use the sidebar to navigate to the detailed type pages.

graphql/boolean.html.title: Boolean
graphql/boolean.html.body:
Boolean scalar
The Boolean scalar type represents true or false.
scalar Boolean

Member Of
include  directive ● skip  directive

graphql/id.html.title: ID
graphql/id.html.body:
ID scalar
The ID scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as "4") or integer (such as 4) input value will be accepted as an ID.
scalar ID

Member Of
CreateIssueInput  input ● Issue  object ● Repository  object ● User  object ● user  query

graphql/int.html.title: Int
graphql/int.html.body:
Int scalar
The Int scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.
scalar Int

Member Of
repositories  query

graphql/string.html.title: String
graphql/string.html.body:
String scalar
The String scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.
scalar String

Member Of
CreateIssueInput  input ● deprecated  directive ● Issue  object ● Repository  object ● specifiedBy  directive ● User  object

_nav/local-directive.html.title: Directives
_nav/local-directive.html.body:
Local Schema - Directives
This page is auto-generated for sidebar grouping.
Use the sidebar to navigate to the detailed type pages.

graphql/deprecated.html.title: deprecated
graphql/deprecated.html.body:
deprecated directive
Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.
directive @deprecated(
  reason: String = "No longer supported"
) on 
  | FIELD_DEFINITION
  | ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
  | INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
  | ENUM_VALUE
  | DIRECTIVE_DEFINITION

Arguments
deprecated.reason ● String scalar
Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by CommonMark.

graphql/include.html.title: include
graphql/include.html.body:
include directive
Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the if argument is true.
directive @include(
  if: Boolean!
) on 
  | FIELD
  | FRAGMENT_SPREAD
  | INLINE_FRAGMENT

Arguments
include.if ● Boolean! non-null scalar
Included when true.

graphql/one-of.html.title: oneOf
graphql/one-of.html.body:
oneOf directive
Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be null.
directive @oneOf on INPUT_OBJECT

graphql/skip.html.title: skip
graphql/skip.html.body:
skip directive
Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the if argument is true.
directive @skip(
  if: Boolean!
) on 
  | FIELD
  | FRAGMENT_SPREAD
  | INLINE_FRAGMENT

Arguments
skip.if ● Boolean! non-null scalar
Skipped when true.

graphql/specified-by.html.title: specifiedBy
graphql/specified-by.html.body:
specifiedBy directive
Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.
directive @specifiedBy(
  url: String!
) on SCALAR

Arguments
specifiedBy.url ● String! non-null scalar
The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

Country object

    lang: String

}

graphql-remote/deprecated.html.title: deprecated
graphql-remote/deprecated.html.body:
deprecated directive
Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.
directive @deprecated(
  reason: String = "No longer supported"
) on 
  | ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
  | ENUM_VALUE
  | FIELD_DEFINITION
  | INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION

Arguments
deprecated.reason ● String scalar
Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by CommonMark.

  | INLINE_FRAGMENT
